entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Commonly known as Arachis hypogaea, used as a parent in intersectional hybridization.

entities.name: ARACHIS DURANENSIS
entities.description: A wild species of Arachis used in hybridization with A. glabrata.

entities.name: ARACHIS DIOGOI
entities.description: A wild species of Arachis used in hybridization with A. glabrata.

entities.name: ARACHIS GLABRATA
entities.description: A wild species of Arachis used as a parent in multiple intersectional hybrids.

entities.name: MEIOSIS
entities.description: The process of cell division involving chromosome pairing and segregation studied in the hybrids.

entities.name: HYBRIDIZATION
entities.description: The process of creating intersectional hybrids between different Arachis species.

entities.name: CHROMOSOME PAIRING
entities.description: The formation of bivalents, trivalents, tetravalents, and univalents during meiotic prophase.

entities.name: GROWTH HABIT
entities.description: The physical growth form of the hybrid, described as erect.

entities.name: HYPANTHIUM MORPHOLOGY
entities.description: The physical characteristic of flower buds, described as having a short hypanthium.

entities.name: FLOWER COLOR
entities.description: The color of the flowers, described as pale yellow.

entities.name: YIELD COMPONENTS
entities.description: The production of pegs, pods, and seeds in the resulting hybrids.

entities.name: CHROMOSOME SEGREGATION
entities.description: The separation of chromosomes during anaphase in the meiotic process.

Hybrid with A. glabrata showed 7-10 bivalents and 10-16 univalents
